---
pagetitle: "Martin Lukk"
description: "Computational social scientist studying how humans and automated systems make decisions about who deserves what."
page-layout: custom
listing:
  - id: news-recent
    contents: news/[0-9]*.qmd
    type: custom
    template: _templates/news-item-recent.ejs
    sort: "date desc"
    fields: [date, title, news_title, hide_in_news, book]
    page-size: 100
---

# About

I am a computational social scientist studying how humans and automated systems make decisions about who deserves what. My research investigates _algorithmic fairness_, _crowdfunding and social welfare_, and _political identity and conflict_.

I co-authored [*GoFailMe: The Unfulfilled Promise of Digital Crowdfunding*](book/) (Stanford University Press) and am developing FairFund-Bench, a benchmark evaluating distributive bias in LLM allocation decisions. I received my Ph.D. in sociology from the University of Toronto.

## News

::: {#news-recent}
:::

[See all News &rarr;](news/){.see-all}
